title: Add project testing mode sessions
summary: Design and implement a foundry workflow mode where an operator specifies a project and starts an isolated testing session whose files, logs, and resume context are saved predictably so later task initiation or continuation can inspect those logs before deciding next work.
acceptance_criteria: the workflow defines an explicit input contract for selecting the project to test
testing-session invocation supports action:start with project:<slug> and optional goal:"..." describing the workflow use or behavior to inspect
testing-session invocation supports action:stop and action:status with session:<session-id>
testing mode is started through a dedicated callable skill slug that can be invoked from the operator surface with a slash-style entry
testing session lifecycle controls are exposed through one canonical callable slug with explicit actions
testing mode is read-only against project source, task JSON, skills, scripts, and normal workflow artifacts
testing mode isolates the session from normal project task execution and makes that boundary visible in task state
a testing session represents the agent actively using the selected project workflows with per-session state logging, not a purely artificial test harness
testing-mode files are saved under projects/<project-slug>/artifacts/testing-sessions/<session-id>/ with deterministic names
each testing session folder contains session.json, events.jsonl, and transcript.md or notes.md
projects/<project-slug>/artifacts/testing-sessions/index.json stores session summaries and pointers rather than full event logs
testing activity logs what ran, what changed inside the isolated testing-session state, what was observed, and what remains unresolved
testing sessions log a structured event stream covering started, preload_complete, decision, tool_run, artifact_read, finding, blocked, and completed events
each testing-session event records timestamp, actor or session id, project slug, task context when present, files read or written, command or tool summary, result status, and rationale
future native Codex planning or native Codex planning flows can load the testing logs and use them to assess what needs to be done next
testing sessions do not produce recommendations, create tasks, continue tasks, or edit normal workflow state by default; they only store the run state
projects/<project-slug>/artifacts/testing-sessions/index.json is the canonical machine-readable testing session index
native Codex planning, native Codex planning, and query-workflow-state can discover the testing session index during project preload
project.json and task context snapshots reference testing-session discovery without duplicating full session logs
validation or fixtures prove testing-mode state is discoverable and does not corrupt normal project trackers
previous workflow style and ECC workflow architecture are preserved throughout the workflow design

context_snapshot.summary: Load workflow-foundry state, every non-done task, previous workflow/ECC references, and task workflow surfaces before shaping project testing mode sessions.

session_log.event: Created intake task after user invoked native Codex planning for foundry testing mode sessions with isolated project selection, durable file saving, and logs that future task flows can preload.

Loaded previous workflow ask-previous workflow and handoff references from /tmp/previous workflowpocock-skills-inspect to preserve the idea-to-workflow and cross-session logging shape before implementation work begins.
Moved to planning after user asked to continue the testing mode task into planning.

Resolved storage location: each testing session should live under projects/<project-slug>/artifacts/testing-sessions/<session-id>/, with additional discoverability from the normal agent preload and discovery surfaces.

Resolved canonical discovery surface: each project should maintain projects/<project-slug>/artifacts/testing-sessions/index.json, and native Codex planning, native Codex planning, and query-workflow-state should discover it during project preload while project.json and task snapshots remain lightweight pointers.

Resolved testing session meaning: testing mode is the agent itself running a project-scoped session, with all relevant agent states logged per session rather than treated as normal implementation work.

Resolved session log state model: testing sessions should write a structured event stream with started, preload_complete, decision, tool_run, artifact_read, finding, blocked, and completed events; each event should include timestamp, actor or session id, project slug, optional task context, files touched inside the isolated testing-session state, command or tool summary, result status, and rationale.

Resolved write boundary and purpose: testing mode must be read-only against project source, task JSON, skills, scripts, and normal workflow artifacts, while still representing real operator use of the workflows with the agent's session states captured for later review.

Resolved invocation surface: testing mode should have a dedicated callable slug, likely testing-session, that the operator can invoke through a slash-style entry while keeping .agents/skills as the canonical workflow surface instead of adding legacy command shims.
Resolved invocation input contract: testing-session should require project:<slug> and accept an optional goal:"..." field that describes the workflow use or behavior to inspect.

Corrected output boundary: testing sessions should not produce recommendations or task actions; the agent runs independently inside the testing session and the durable output is only the captured run state.
Resolved stop/control surface: testing-session lifecycle controls, including stopping the independent run, should be exposed through explicit callable slugs rather than implicit normal task flow.

Resolved lifecycle command shape: use one canonical testing-session slug with explicit actions such as action:start project:<slug> goal:"...", action:stop session:<session-id>, and action:status session:<session-id>.

Resolved testing-session file layout: each session folder should contain session.json for metadata/status, events.jsonl for structured events, and transcript.md or notes.md for human-readable context; the project-level testing-sessions/index.json should store summaries and pointers only.
Wrote PRD for project testing sessions and moved task to planning with issues as the next action.

Published five local issue artifacts for testing-session start, status/stop lifecycle, preload discovery, validation fixtures, and read-only live-use documentation; moved task to work-item planning with implement as the next action.
Approved implementation artifacts for the testing-session skill, state helper, query/validation integration, preflight skill updates, and lifecycle tests; moved task to implementation.

Implemented testing-session lifecycle state helper, canonical testing-session skill, project preload discovery, validation checks, tracker tests, and issue acceptance updates; moved task to code-review.
Completed code-review with no blocking findings after spec review, workflow validation, tracker verification, skill validation, whitespace check, and secret-pattern scan; marked task done.
